# [Why the Hell Are You Running for Office If You Don't Want to Solve Problems?](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4258115&post_id=167742553&utm_source=post-email-title&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY3NzQyNTUzLCJpYXQiOjE3NTE5MDk4NjgsImV4cCI6MTc1NDUwMTg2OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQyNTgxMTUiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.IcGZvlmE84-8zFz_Eupk1l3n06JzGk4u32OYAwmD5XM)
### A Montana library fight, a broken government, and the urgent need for leaders who actually give a damn.
| | [Mike Nellis](https://substack.com/@mikenellis)
---
| Jul 7
---
| [](https://substack.com/@mikenellis)
---

---
| | | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4258115&post_id=167742553&utm_source=substack&isFreemail=true&submitLike=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY3NzQyNTUzLCJyZWFjdGlvbiI6IuKdpCIsImlhdCI6MTc1MTkwOTg2OCwiZXhwIjoxNzU0NTAxODY4LCJpc3MiOiJwdWItNDI1ODExNSIsInN1YiI6InJlYWN0aW9uIn0.ZeNIZsktPqwY_Kz15WboQrWvjbp8lAKORr5QmwMm_xw&utm_medium=email&utm_campaign=email-reaction&r=1hvr60)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4258115&post_id=167742553&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&isFreemail=true&comments=true&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY3NzQyNTUzLCJpYXQiOjE3NTE5MDk4NjgsImV4cCI6MTc1NDUwMTg2OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQyNTgxMTUiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.IcGZvlmE84-8zFz_Eupk1l3n06JzGk4u32OYAwmD5XM&r=1hvr60&utm_campaign=email-half-magic-comments&action=post-comment&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [](https://substack.com/app-link/post?publication_id=4258115&post_id=167742553&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email&utm_content=share&utm_campaign=email-share&action=share&triggerShare=true&isFreemail=true&r=1hvr60&token=eyJ1c2VyX2lkIjo5MDUwMTE5MiwicG9zdF9pZCI6MTY3NzQyNTUzLCJpYXQiOjE3NTE5MDk4NjgsImV4cCI6MTc1NDUwMTg2OCwiaXNzIjoicHViLTQyNTgxMTUiLCJzdWIiOiJwb3N0LXJlYWN0aW9uIn0.IcGZvlmE84-8zFz_Eupk1l3n06JzGk4u32OYAwmD5XM)
---
| | [](https://substack..TlBg37_Q10NmD-6zpLfUZ0Ol_Td67F3oC_gjFagPzE4?&utm_source=substack&utm_medium=email)
---
| | [READ IN APP](https://open.substack.com/pub/endlessurgency/p/why-the-hell-are-you-running-for?utm_source=email&redirect=app-store&utm_campaign=email-read-in-app)
---

I'm getting back into the swing of things after taking my family to Montana for summer vacation last week. Obviously, I'm going to have a lot of things to say about the budget the Republicans passed this weekend, but first I want to stop and reflect on a local story I came across while I was in Montana.
One of the things I loved about being outside the Big City was indulging in everything Montana had to offer--from incredible national parks to family-owned diners to reading the local newspaper at the coffee shop instead of scrolling on my damn phone. At one stop along Route 93, I read a story in the local newspaper about a library on Flathead Lake that ran a program offering 50 internet hotspots people could rent when they needed them. The program existed because of a federal grant--until the grant was cut, I assume, thanks to Elon Musk and DOGE.
The community was understandably upset and wanted the library to find a way to keep the program alive. The journalist who wrote the piece spoke to the head of the library board--it seemed like an elected position, but I'm not sure--and asked if they'd keep the program going. And the guy in charge said two things that really stuck with me, in the worst way:
First, he said he didn't believe in government welfare and didn't think the government should be providing hotspots to people. He was fundamentally opposed to the program and didn't think it was his job to provide rural broadband access. Second--and this is the part that really lit me up--without a shred of evidence, he suggested that most people using the hotspots were just taking them into the mountains to watch porn. Completely ridiculous.
The article also mentioned that some folks in the community had offered to raise funds to keep the program going. But the library board rejected that too, saying it wasn't the library's job to provide internet. And to me, this is everything wrong with how so many people--especially the Republicans, but far too many Democrats too--approach government.
They're not even trying to solve problems.
_I 'm committed to keeping Endless Urgency free for everyone--but I rely on paid subscribers (starting at just $8/month) to keep it going. If you find value in the work, consider becoming a paid subscriber._
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..NzRpvBVUMoMjI7tpsAZCuBkhgBuicpgdcWJjA26wCtc?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=167742553)
I don't care if you're running for dog catcher or President of the United States--your job is to improve people's lives. Every moment we move away from that--where we're either hurting people or standing still--is a failure. And this guy who doesn't think the library's job is to provide access to information? Give me a break. The whole point of a library is access--to books, to education, to learning. And yes, the internet is part of that now.
Even if it's not "technically" the library's job to fix rural broadband, it is still a public good and a point of access to service. You can still lead. You can govern. You can try. You can at least create a bridge until bigger solutions come along. And having driven all across Montana, I can tell you: internet access out there is rough in large parts of the state. And people are getting left behind because of it.
The article shared stories of people relying on those hotspots to apply for jobs, to book doctor's appointments. The role of government should be to give people opportunities to move forward. And if that means you can get online at a library to find a job, that's exactly the kind of thing we should be supporting.
What's broken in government, at every level, is that too many people care more about being right or giving off the appearance of getting things done than actually getting things done--to the detriment of public welfare. Too many people climbing the ladder, chasing power, influence, money. We have a generation of politicians who seem to only care about how they personally profit rather than how they serve.
That's how Donald Trump, Mike Johnson, and the Republican Party passed such a horrendous budget this weekend--because of all the so-called moderate Republicans who pretended to be on the fence just long enough to squeeze out favors or photo ops or whatever else. Transactional politics. Meanwhile, the bill itself? It does nothing to improve people's lives. It helps billionaires--makes tax cuts for the wealthiest permanent while the tax relief for regular folks is limited and temporary. It makes an investment in corporate America at the expense of working-class America, with little to no return.
And what we don't have--what we desperately need--are politicians who just want to solve problems. Who see people struggling to pay bills, find housing, get a job, or just get online, and are actively trying to do something about it. We need elected representatives to be bold, relentless in their pursuit of addressing the needs of The People, and who are collaborative and cooperative in finding solutions.
Which brings me back to the core question: Why the fuck are you running for office if you aren't trying to solve problems?
_I 'm committed to keeping Endless Urgency free for everyone--but I rely on paid subscribers (starting at just $8/month) to keep it going. If you find value in the work, consider becoming a paid subscriber._
[Upgrade to paid](https://substack..NzRpvBVUMoMjI7tpsAZCuBkhgBuicpgdcWJjA26wCtc?&utm_medium=email&utm_source=subscribe-widget-preamble&utm_content=167742553)
Call me naive, but if you hold elected office--if you're a government worker, a member of a community, a person who gives a shit about others--you should wake up every day trying to make your little corner of the world better. Not passing the buck. Not looking for excuses. Trying.
To the Library Board member, to the GOP, to Trump, to all those in places of power: You are elected to represent and be of service to those constituencies who democratically deemed you qualified to protect and advance their well-being, safety, and interests. When we elect you to carry out the sacred duty of representing us, we are making an investment in you, and we should, therefore, see a tangible return on that investment. When that stops happening, change is necessary.
That's what's corrosively broken in our politics right now: too few people with any real interest in getting things done for working people. Too much ideological rigidity. Too much cold-heartedness toward people who need help. And on top of that? Way too much assuming the worst of people. That's broken too.
The guy who doesn't want to fund library hotspots assumes people are using them to watch porn instead of apply for jobs. The folks voting to cut Medicaid for 20 million Americans assume that people on Medicaid are lazy. We assume the worst, and that's just wrong.
We need government--and community leaders--that assume the best (more Leslie Knopes, less Selina Meyers). Leaders who believe in people. Who try to give folks a shot to succeed. Because at the end of the day, the vast majority of people just want to feel valued. They want a good-paying job. A chance to start a family. To buy a house. To retire with dignity. And that's true no matter who you are.
People are going to gravitate toward leaders who demonstrate--both in words and in actions--that they actually give a shit about them. Leaders who will try to make your life better. Leaders who will actually get something done and are not afraid to be bold, change gears, and take risks to improve the lives of everyday people.
And that's who I'll be watching for in the next year and a half to see how emerges as we build this next generation of American leadership together--whether it's Zohran Mamdani in New York City, Mallory McMorrow in Michigan, Lauren Underwood in the Chicago area, or anybody else.

name: Byron Donalds
subject: I just finished meeting with President Trump
body:
The age of illegal aliens overrunning our country is over.

| | | | |
---
[](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z7t5obh1hkim4u088ha43ul538eioln6qqm1qpb8_rp22sh2s8i6epb4cjh6co72cdh2bs)
|
---
_**I just finished my meeting with President Trump.**_
It’s his STRONGEST supporter in Florida, Byron Donalds, and I want to share with you what we discussed:
**Alligator Alcatraz.**
Radical Democrats’ open border policies for far too long have endangered EVERY American citizen. These policies are _UNACCEPTABLE_ and _DANGEROUS_.
Alligator Alcatraz will be the **LARGEST illegal detention center EVER** , and it will be sitting deep in the Everglades, preventing any chance of escape.
But the Democrats, including the man spending millions against me to flip Florida BLUE, are doing EVERYTHING possible to _DESTROY_ Alligator Alcatraz and put illegal immigrants ahead of American citizens.
They’re raising MILLIONS of dollars with the promise to _END deportations_.
I won’t let that happen and I know you won’t either. Your response, right here, right now- _**[will send a message loud and clear that MAGA Patriots stand with President Trump and MAGA’s fight to save our nation from illegal immigration.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zn2tgjet6417lp89e3b1u83p27giuhmv1l3fc16g_rp22sh2s8i6epb4cjh6co72cdh2bs)**_

|
---
[STAND WITH TRUMP](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2zb3b8s3d1v3t4jvb2dh10epmiqe256pb8g7ssbno_rp22sh2s8i6epb4cjh6co72cdh2bs)

|
---
As the next Governor of Florida, I will enact President Trump’s America First agenda to the _LETTER_ and protect Floridians from illegal aliens.
_**[But I WON’T be able to win this fight on my own.](http://e.americanactionnews.com/rd/9z2z4lmgnavkgj2qr9006g53engokvhthvdiblo2dc0_rp22sh2s8i6epb4cjh6co72cdh2bs)**_

God bless you,
Byron Donalds

subject: The greatest armed conflict in human history
body:
This fact means that we’re losing more and more living memory of the greatest armed conflict

This sponsored message from Your America is on behalf of Hillsdale College
[](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zgi0hrcehjhfn878m9q3go4f8vj5t5j62b16np98_rp22sh2s8i6epb4cjh6co72cdh2bs) Dear Fellow American,
Only a fraction of the 16 million Americans who fought bravely in World War II remain with us. This fact means that we’re losing more and more living memory of the greatest armed conflict in human history, which makes the study of World War II even more important.
To this end, Hillsdale College has produced an online course called **“The Second World Wars.”**
**Taught by historian Victor Davis Hanson** , this course explores how the conflict’s diverse theaters, belligerents, and ways of fighting came eventually to define a single war. And it examines the key investments, strategies, and leaders that led the Allied powers to victory over the Axis. [](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zig06o8dmp8t9eechujkri092ibpos7nle6t7bf0_rp22sh2s8i6epb4cjh6co72cdh2bs) If you’d like to secure a DVD set before they’re all gone, you can get one shipped directly to your home for any gift of $100 or more to Hillsdale College.
Here’s a secure link to reserve your copy of **“The Second World Wars”** on DVD:
**[https://secured.hillsdale.edu/hillsdale/support-the-second-world-wars](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zgb26u5v05c348ilr68kl45uj62t3ej3gbb1knvg_rp22sh2s8i6epb4cjh6co72cdh2bs)**
Thank you for preserving liberty for future generations of Americans through education with Hillsdale College!
Best regards,
Jeremiah Regan, Ph.D. Executive Director of Online Learning Hillsdale College
P.S. The **“The Second World Wars”** DVD set is great as a homeschool curriculum or for independent study. Plus, it makes a perfect gift for anyone who wants to know more about the Second World War. **[Reserve your copy now.](https://e.youramerica.news/rd/9z2zgnmnkmk6pka31e6r9nc4v4qiaeqm5i81qjpm2to_rp22sh2s8i6epb4cjh6co72cdh2bs)**

name: Terence Sears
subject: Let me tell you a little bit about my wife, Winsome!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Winsome for Governor
body:
Winsome’s opponent has millions in dark money

[](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2ze2bit6fg3r2lrnnpiijic0hjqedfvgpij7b9d6g_rp22sh2s8i6epb4cjh6aor2c1h2bs) Friend, I'm Terence Sears. My wife is Winsome Earle-Sears. If you haven't met her yet, she is a conservative Christian running for Governor against a George Soros-backed liberal. [](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zinmai1146c31hadq50f6qf554fir6n3p7n5eg2g_rp22sh2s8i6epb4cjh6aor2c1h2bs) Winsome's opponent has millions in dark money, but the race is still TIED. That's the power of my wife's positive message that she's spreading!
Winsome's dad immigrated to America with $1.75 in his pocket and brought his family over, including a young Winsome, once he established himself. They were able to make ends meet here, and live the American Dream they came to achieve.
Because she was black, she spent a lot of her life thinking she was a Democrat. But when George Bush, Sr. was running for president, she heard him say that if all you ever have is welfare, you will never have anything to pass on to your children.
Now, she is a leading conservative voice, spreading her message about the American Dream and the opportunities created by the Republican Party.
Listen, Winsome has won with her positive message & no-quit attitude before. I see her fight every day and I know she can do it again. Her Democrat opponent has absurd amounts of money with more pouring in from George Soros-backed groups and Hollywood Leftists every day. I can promise you this: Winsome needs you in her corner to win this crucial race! [Today, I'm personally asking you to step up with a donation of $5, $10, or even $25 to support the love of my life in the most important election of 2025. Please chip in now:](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2z7p36udnu6js9ekgnhie549tkbnn1ae4corhmucg_rp22sh2s8i6epb4cjh6aor2c1h2bs) [ I'LL SUPPORT WINSOME!
](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zduf7ar1rt6tj6klgascs6c97t9l7fmndqnmi6jg_rp22sh2s8i6epb4cjh6aor2c1h2bs) Every penny you send in today will go toward Winsome's fight. It'll be spent on events, TV ads, getting out the vote, and campaign signs. I can't understate how critical all of these things are in our fight. I hope you'll chip in today and ensure the best wife and mother a guy could ever ask for becomes Virginia's next Governor! [ CHIP IN NOW >>
](http://e.1600daily.com/rd/9z2zh76r490emaps4o4ck5rogn8vfks9me4r40ins38_rp22sh2s8i6epb4cjh6aor2c1h2bs) Thanks so much and may God bless you!
-Terence Sears

name: Deb Haaland
subject: This is the truth about breaking barriers:
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR AND AUTHORIZED BY DEB FOR NEW MEXICO
body:
No state in America has ever elected a Native woman as governor.
# [](https://act.debhaaland.com/go/133?t=1&akid=3080%2E400544%2E_Kg965)
I’m a proud member of the Pueblo of Laguna. I was a military kid and raised an amazing child as a single mom. I’m an auntie, a sister, and a public servant.
But I’m not supposed to be here.
Our country was built on principles and systems meant to either assimilate or exterminate people like me. Either to make me blend into the background of America, or get rid of me altogether.
_But, against all odds, I am still here._
I’m deeply proud of the opportunities I had to break barriers, first in Congress and most recently as the 54th Secretary of the Interior. But every day under this administration, we are reminded that our fight for true representation, equity, and justice is far from over.
**No state in America has ever elected a Native woman as governor.[Can you make your first $5 contribution to our history-making campaign for New Mexico?](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250707_1_native?amount=5&refcode=em20250707-3080&refcode2=3080_400544__Kg965&akid=3080%2E400544%2E_Kg965)**
**[Donate $5 »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250707_1_native?amount=5&refcode=em20250707-3080&refcode2=3080_400544__Kg965&akid=3080%2E400544%2E_Kg965)**
When I was growing up, there weren’t many women in elected office, and even fewer Indigenous people. How was I to know my path was even possible?
By running a campaign that was brave, fierce, and rooted in the power of community, I was able to stand on the shoulders of my ancestors and prove to little girls of color everywhere that they too belong everywhere decisions are being made.
[](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250707_1_native?refcode=em20250707-3080&refcode2=3080_400544__Kg965&akid=3080%2E400544%2E_Kg965)
_After I was elected, I had the honor of being photographed by Shane Balkowitsch, who specializes in a historic style of wet plate photography._
This is why I believe representation matters. And why I think every single one of us has a role to play in taking the power of that representation to the next level.
[**Together, we can make history in this election. Please chip in $5 or any amount you can afford today so we can build the strong, well-resourced campaign it will take to win this race.**](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_20250707_1_native?amount=5&refcode=em20250707-3080&refcode2=3080_400544__Kg965&akid=3080%2E400544%2E_Kg965)
Be fierce,
Deb
**[Donate »](https://secure.actblue.com/donate/em_ms_dh_footer?refcode=em20250707-1&t=2&refcode2=3080_400544__Kg965&akid=3080%2E400544%2E_Kg965)**

name: Randy Villegas
subject: In case you missed it: Valadao lied. Votes to cut Medicaid.
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Villegas for Congress Villegas for Congress
body:
|
#
I'm making sure you saw this news, team. **My Republican opponent David Valadao just broke his promise and voted for Trump’s budget -- slashing nearly $1 trillion from Medicaid and gutting critical services for thousands of working families here in the Central Valley.** [****](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111044640/556037481/1981932150?refcode=em-7.7.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTc1MjUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYzdlYmY4MmItNWQ1Yi1mMDExLThmN2MtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=AdWfGLio0MAqWH6kv8Xn-50m_YBYFPz9Kc9iQ6FizU8=) _**Nearly 70% of our district relies on Medi-Cal.**_ That includes my family, students, and nearly everyone I grew up with. _Valadao knows this._ That’s what makes this vote so spineless, shameful, and so personal. Just months ago, Valadao promised he would never support a bill that cuts Medicaid for vulnerable communities. He even put it in writing. **Well, he lied. ** Valadao showed us exactly who he works for: billionaires and party bosses, not the people he was elected to represent. **This is one of the most devastating betrayals in our district’s history. And in the district hardest-hit by this bill in the entire country -- it could cost lives.** I’m in this race for the working people who’ve been ignored and exploited for too long. Valadao's vote cannot go unpunished, and together we can kick him out for good. **[Valadao has never been more vulnerable, and we have our best shot to flip this seat. Please rush a donation of $5, $35, or whatever you can. Let’s make this the moment we turn outrage into action -- and show Valadao that betraying the Central Valley comes at a price.](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111044641/556037482/1981932150?refcode=em-7.7.25-all-a&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTc1MjUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYzdlYmY4MmItNWQ1Yi1mMDExLThmN2MtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=AdWfGLio0MAqWH6kv8Xn-50m_YBYFPz9Kc9iQ6FizU8=)** __If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: __ | | | [Chip in $10 Now](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111044642/556037483/1981932150?refcode=em-7.7.25-all-a&express_lane=true&amount=10&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNjIvMS8xMTc1MjUiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiYzdlYmY4MmItNWQ1Yi1mMDExLThmN2MtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=AdWfGLio0MAqWH6kv8Xn-50m_YBYFPz9Kc9iQ6FizU8=)

clean_body: | I'm making sure you saw this news, team. My Republican opponent David Valadao just broke his promise and voted for Trump’s budget -- slashing nearly $1 trillion from Medicaid and gutting critical services for thousands of working families here in the Central Valley. = Nearly 70% of our district relies on Medi-Cal. That includes my family, students, and nearly everyone I grew up with. Valadao knows this. That’s what makes this vote so spineless, shameful, and so personal. Just months ago, Valadao promised he would never support a bill that cuts Medicaid for vulnerable communities. He even put it in writing. Well, he lied. Valadao showed us exactly who he works for: billionaires and party bosses, not the people he was elected to represent. This is one of the most devastating betrayals in our district’s history. And in the district hardest-hit by this bill in the entire country -- it could cost lives. I’m in this race for the working people who’ve been ignored and exploited for too long. Valadao's vote cannot go unpunished, and together we can kick him out for good. = If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately: | | | = | = | | = | = | | = | = Let’s send a message that we won’t back down. In this fight, \- Randy | With deep ties to our community and a lifelong commitment to justice and equity, Randy is running for Congress in California’s 22nd District to bring bold, compassionate leadership to Washington. To support Randy's campaign = We'd hate to see you go, but you can click here if you'd like to . Paid for by Villegas for Congress Villegas for Congress PO Box 1346 Visalia, CA 93279 United States

name: Kotek HQ
subject: Re: the budget
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Tina Kotek
body:
Republicans voted to kick 17 million Americans off their health care coverage. |
#
---
Peter, Tina has been making clear for months how devastating the Republican budget will be for Oregon families. Rural hospitals and nursing homes could be forced to close, hundreds of thousands of Oregonians will lose health care coverage, energy bills will increase, and working families will struggle to feed their children. This budget was inked in greed, not human dignity — all to appease their billionaire donors. Their justification? _“We are all going to die”_ and _“They’ll get over it.”_ Tina and our team are going to keep fighting, every day, for the 1 in 3 Oregonians who rely on Medicaid and SNAP. But there’s one thing you can do right now: **[Add your name to show Congressional Republicans how much the American people hate this bill – and how they’ll have to pay for their votes come 2026:](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111045097/556039072/413995129?refcode=AU_TK_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20250707_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZmRmYjEyMS01ZTViLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DI4waQEbFbm9s2tPDc3dcyIHazvn0HL1lmAp6wl9Z48=&emci=47f590ef-4f5b-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=8fdfb121-5e5b-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=15353729)** | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111045098/556039073/413995129?refcode=AU_TK_EG_GEN_PET_AC_20250707_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMTIvMS85MjY1NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4ZmRmYjEyMS01ZTViLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=DI4waQEbFbm9s2tPDc3dcyIHazvn0HL1lmAp6wl9Z48=&emci=47f590ef-4f5b-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=8fdfb121-5e5b-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=15353729)

name: Team Davis
subject: Read this if you're ready to fight back
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Austin Davis
body:
Austin Davis is helping continue the progress we've made for Pennsylvanians by ensuring we keep Pennsylvania blue up and down the ballot. | | **What's happening in Congress right now is a complete disaster. **Republicans just passed a bill that will strip millions of their healthcare, with the most significant cut to Medicaid in history. Rural hospitals will be forced to close their doors. People won't be able to access the care they need to live. It's despicable, Peter. Food assistance has also been slashed, and on top of that, free school lunch now faces additional barriers. **They're taking food out of kids' mouths to fund tax breaks for the ultra-wealthy. ** **We need to show national Republicans that this bill is NOT what Pennsylvanians want. It's bad for the Commonwealth and bad for the country. ** [**That’s why we’re hoping to collect 1,000 signatures today so we can send the resounding message that Pennsylvanians do NOT support Trump's bill →**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111045102/556039106/433170734?source=SBD_AD_EM_ENG_070725_BILL_30DO30MO_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NzIyMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmODUwNmQ2Ni01ZTViLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ooyscoCtgS1qOxlpKeiM_QaIb2F5Dj-QYKu5FEgMpso=&emci=f4506d66-5e5b-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=f8506d66-5e5b-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=31337995) | | [ADD YOUR NAME](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111045103/556039107/433170734?source=SBD_AD_EM_ENG_070725_BILL_30DO30MO_D1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NzIyMyIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmODUwNmQ2Ni01ZTViLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=ooyscoCtgS1qOxlpKeiM_QaIb2F5Dj-QYKu5FEgMpso=&emci=f4506d66-5e5b-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=f8506d66-5e5b-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=31337995)

name: Jason C. Johnson
subject: Another New Mexico cop charged with murder
body:
A dangerous pattern that will quickly lead to less policing and an increase in violence and crime in communities.

| |
---
| | That’s not the momentum we want heading into a busy spring in the courtroom.
---
| |
---
[](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2z7i2c6fbpudrtvngicn7ivb5hrdieuc7a8k3f5m8_rp22sh2s8i6epb4cjh66pb4c1h2bs)
---
Dear Friend, New Mexico is making a name for itself, but not in a way you’d expect.This year, in addition to the politically motivated prosecution of Officer Brad Lunsford, there is yet _another_ New Mexico police officer being wrongfully charged and this time it’s for **_first-degree_ _murder_.**An anti-police pattern is clearly emerging and corrupting New Mexico’s justice system.A dangerous pattern that will quickly lead to less policing and an increase in violence and crime in communities.[**Which is why I need to ask for your support today.**](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zqot0g02knqh2l8576ia47nh0ij48qf1gpmgnvng_rp22sh2s8i6epb4cjh66pb4c1h2bs)
---
[](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zinn53ki3sj8cfdioa3dop1pvpufiq84u2ja27t0_rp22sh2s8i6epb4cjh66pb4c1h2bs)
---
Let me introduce you to Deputy Jacob Diaz, his fiancée (who is also a deputy), and their 3-year-old son in the picture I’ve sent you.Jacob is a three-year veteran of the Otero County Sheriff’s Office, and he grew up in a law enforcement family proudly serving their community.Now, he faces life in prison for doing his duty and following his training.Friend, this charge is shocking, and even ridiculous once you know the facts.In June 2024, Deputy Jacob Diaz responded to a late-night report of a young man walking in an intoxicated manner along the median of a busy highway.When Diaz arrived on the scene, the individual repeatedly ignored his instructions to stay back and show his hands.Instead, the young man quickly walked towards the deputy and drew out what appeared to be a firearm raising it shoulder high. In response, Deputy Diaz followed his training and fired his weapon.Even after falling to the ground, the individual continued to ignore the officer’s order to remain still and started moving towards the perceived firearm. Once again, following his training and fearing for his life, Deputy Diaz discharged his firearm and the individual was killed.An administrative investigation on behalf of the New Mexico State Police was completed and submitted – **finding Deputy Diaz had followed his training and had sufficient cause** to stop the young man and defend himself after the perceived threat to his life. And because the investigation cleared him, Jacob was reinstated as an officer on the force.**_But out of the blue, an anti-police prosecutor charged Deputy Diaz with first-degree murder._****** You can imagine the absolute shock Jacob and his fiancée had when receiving the news. After four months of being cleared of any fault and being back on the force, if convicted, he faces life in prison.Tearing a 3-year-old little boy away from his father for the rest of his life.Friend, that’s something no child should have to endure. But this isn’t the first time a good officer in New Mexico has been targeted with a false charge. You will remember Soros-funded Attorney General Jose Garza is trying to lock up Officer Brad Lunsford under phony charges of manslaughter.These politically motivated attacks against our men and women in blue must stop. That’s why the Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund (LELDF) has committed to supporting Deputy Diaz in his fight for justice.[**As a faithful supporter of law enforcement, can I count on you to renew your support and stand with Deputy Jacob Diaz?**](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2z9sr0bqu3ucacjdiop7nqgsf54ufamndle2kskcg_rp22sh2s8i6epb4cjh66pb4c1h2bs) Your donation of $35 or more will help provide:
---
* Expert witnesses on use of force
* The best attorneys available
* And cover any other court expenses Deputy Diaz will be faced with

---
| | Because without your support, even if Jacob wins his trial, he and his loved ones will be left with astronomical court fees and crippling debt. Everyday our officers put their lives on the line when they go out to protect our streets, just like Deputy Diaz did that night in June. And if we don’t support them, what will happen to our communities?We already know exactly what will happen – just look at the current state of L.A. Our country is once again being faced with a decision – will we be a nation of law and order or a nation of lawlessness and disorder.I pray that you continue to stand by our nation’s values and those who defend them.
---
Sincerely,
---
---
Jason C. JohnsonPresident, Law Enforcement Legal Defense Fund
---
[ **DONATE** ](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2zub8iq795rdrbk5g14hkdrhru73ln9ftsk9sg2po_rp22sh2s8i6epb4cjh66pb4c1h2bs)
---
| | _More such officers who sacrifice so much for will face similar fates unless we speak out and stand up for the brave men and women in blue. The next time you see a police officer in your community, please thank them for their service. To read more about our mission or the other Law Enforcement officers we're defending, please visit www.leldf.org. And remember - Blue Lives Matter!_ [**Join our sustaining members by making a monthly gift to LELDF today!**](https://e.updatesforgreatamericans.com/rd/9z2z73gar4hkf2492pghh5cmkbeifg4ou5q24ko0gjo_rp22sh2s8i6epb4cjh66pb4c1h2bs)
---

name: Austin Chenge
subject: My 10years Record Supporting President Trump's Big, Bold Ideas
body:

| | My 10years Record supporting President Trump's Big, Bold Ideas. "To those who laughed and mocked me for supporting President Trump when he announced his candidacy in June 2015, those who mocked my prediction of his 2016 presidential victory, and those who cut ties with me for endorsing policies like the Travel Ban; to those, including Representative Ilhan Omar, Charlamagne Tha God, and others, who viciously attacked me during my campaign for Governor of Michigan to end wokeness and enact bold reforms: I’m still here. I’m still standing and supporting transformative change at the Presidency and fighting for same, for my home State of Michigan." Austin Chenge, US Army Veteran and Former Republican Candidate for Governor of Michigan.

| | For Immediate ReleaseJuly 07, 2025 – Grand Rapids, Michigan Austin Chenge Stands Firm on his Record of over 10yrs support for President Trump's Big Bold Ideas. Grand Rapids, MI – Austin Chenge, a steadfast supporter of President Donald J. Trump and a bold advocate for change, reaffirms his unwavering resolve in the face of adversity. To those who laughed and mocked me for supporting President Trump when he announced his candidacy in June 2015, those who mocked my prediction of his 2016 presidential victory, and those who cut ties with me for endorsing policies like the Travel Ban; to those, including Representative Ilhan Omar, Charlamagne Tha God, and others, who viciously attacked me during my campaign for Governor of Michigan to end wokeness and enact bold reforms: I’m still here. I’m still standing and supporting transformative change at the Presidency and fighting for same for my home State of Michigan. Reflecting on his early support for Trump since June 2015, Chenge’s foresight is evident with posts like his October 21, 2016, assertion: “Silent Trump supporters are the ones who will hand Hillary Clinton a shocking defeat. It really doesn’t matter how many polls the mainstream media produce in her favor...” This was followed by his November 9, 2016, statement after the election: “Yes! Donald Trump Wins Historic US election. Sometimes, we the little people can take on the establishment and win.” His prescient views continued as he celebrated Trump’s Travel Ban victory, noting on June 26, 2017, “As I wrote back in February 2017, that Trump will have the last laugh, well the Supreme Court just handed Trump a huge victory on the Travel Ban.” Despite facing criticism and personal attacks, Chenge remained undeterred. “No dream is too big. No challenge is too great. Nothing we want for our future is beyond our reach,” he echoed, aligning with Trump’s vision, propelling him to the National spotlight with his 2020 Campaign for Governor of Michigan. Chenge’s journey, marked by resilience and determination, continues to position him as a leader ready to support and advocate for transformative change both at the Presidency and in his Home State of Michigan. Austin ChengeVeteran and Former Republican Candidate for Governor of MichiganAlma Mater: The University of Birmingham Law School, England.W: www.austinchenge.com Note: This press release was issued by The Office of Austin Chenge.
---

name: Pramila Jayapal
subject: If the White House doesn’t want ICE to be compared to a terrorist force, they should stop acting like one.
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY JUSTICE DEMOCRATS
body:
[1]Justice Democrats

Peter, I went on CNN to tell the
truth about ICE: They’re acting like a terrorist force, with masked men
jumping out of unmarked cars to kidnap, detain, and disappear people
without explanation.

It’s unconstitutional, illegal, and they’re terrorizing innocent people.

What I said is the truth, and it clearly struck a nerve. Because now the
White House is calling me “deranged” for standing up for immigrants:

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250707-1-pj-partner-email-justicedemocrats?refcode=em20250707-36822&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36822_1203867_7314s_&akid=36822%2E1203867%2E7314s_ ]CNN graphic showing the White House and a quote from White House
spokeswoman Abigail Jackson. The quote reads: 'Dangerous smears by
deranged leftists like Jayapal radicalize their supporters to violently
attack and obstruct federal law enforcement...' The lower banner says:
'IMMIGRATION CRACKDOWN: WHITE HOUSE BLASTS 'DISGUSTING COMMENTS' FROM REP.
JAYAPAL ON ICE.'

Let me be clear: I will not be intimidated, and I am never going to stop
fighting for the rights and dignity of every person, everywhere,
regardless of their immigration status.

The White House can come after me all they want, but if they don’t want
ICE to be compared to a terrorist force, they should stop acting like one.
Period.

Republicans are trying to silence me, but with your support, they won’t
succeed. [ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250707-1-pj-partner-email-justicedemocrats?refcode=em20250707-36822&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36822_1203867_7314s_&akid=36822%2E1203867%2E7314s_ ]Will you split a donation between me and Justice Democrats now
to help me keep standing up against Trump’s radical right-wing immigration
policy?

[ https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jd-em-20250707-1-pj-partner-email-justicedemocrats?refcode=em20250707-36822&amounts=10%2C27%2C50%2C100%2C250%2C500%2C1000&amount=5&refcode2=36822_1203867_7314s_&akid=36822%2E1203867%2E7314s_ ]Contribute $5

In solidarity,

Pramila Jayapal

Contribute to Justice Democrats: https://secure.actblue.com/donate/justicedemocrats?refcode=em20250707-36822-footer&t=1002&refcode2=36822_1203867_7314s_&akid=36822%2E1203867%2E7314s_

name: Mallory McMorrow
subject: This isn’t my first rodeo
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the Ohio House Democratic Campaign Committee, 545 E Town St
body:
I’m no easy target.

|
---
Hi, this is Mallory McMorrow. Mike Rogers, the leading Michigan Republican candidate for Senate, is already attacking me by name. He said, _“McMorrow is an easy target.”**Really?**_ Am I an easy target because I flipped a red district blue? Am I an easy target because I’m building a broad coalition across the state in support of a better vision for Michigan? Am I an easy target because I represent a new generation of leadership that people are desperately craving? Because I’m not the same old politician that Rogers is used to? **Listen, I don’t care about what Mike Rogers says about me. I hope it helped him sleep well last night. I hope he brought in a few dollars. He’s just another talking head for the Trump agenda. ** [**If you care about fighting Trump’s extreme agenda and sending a new kind of leader to the Senate, will you split a donation between Ohio House Dems and my campaign right now? **](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111045972/556042241/-1576555546?refcode=250707_McMorrow_About&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMDYvMS84NDI2NiIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJmMWE5M2ExMi02MTViLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=xFX8wT_IEO3hvZ2jhdlgp1s-u3VVTS0qdtWult7rTtI=) This isn’t my first rodeo as a punching bag for the GOP. In my first campaign in 2018, they sent out a mailer with a picture of me drinking a margarita by a pool, with a curling iron plastered across the width of it that said, “Vote No On Mallory McMorrow.” You know what we did in response? _We won. _ This is just the beginning of the Republican attacks against me. The GOP clearly views me as a threat — and it’s not surprising to see why. Every time they’ve attacked me, they’ve only raised my profile — and we’ve responded by organizing, fighting back, and winning. Every. Single. Time. But clearly they still think this works. Let’s show ‘em they’re wrong. Again. [**Will you stand with me by splitting a contribution between my campaign for Senate and Ohio House Dems today? clean_body: I’m no easy target. | Hi, this is Mallory McMorrow. Mike Rogers, the leading Michigan Republican candidate for Senate, is already attacking me by name. He said, “McMorrow is an easy target.”Really? Am I an easy target because I flipped a red district blue? Am I an easy target because I’m building a broad coalition across the state in support of a better vision for Michigan? Am I an easy target because I represent a new generation of leadership that people are desperately craving? Because I’m not the same old politician that Rogers is used to? Listen, I don’t care about what Mike Rogers says about me. I hope it helped him sleep well last night. I hope he brought in a few dollars. He’s just another talking head for the Trump agenda. = This isn’t my first rodeo as a punching bag for the GOP. In my first campaign in 2018, they sent out a mailer with a picture of me drinking a margarita by a pool, with a curling iron plastered across the width of it that said, “Vote No On Mallory McMorrow.” You know what we did in response? We won. This is just the beginning of the Republican attacks against me. The GOP clearly views me as a threat — and it’s not surprising to see why. Every time they’ve attacked me, they’ve only raised my profile — and we’ve responded by organizing, fighting back, and winning. Every. Single. Time. But clearly they still think this works. Let’s show ‘em they’re wrong. Again. = | | = "" | = "" | = "" | = "" Thank you, Mallory McMorrow = = = OHDC 545 E. As we look ahead to the next phase of this campaign, I want to know where your head is at. New Mexicans — and grassroots supporters across the country — are facing big challenges: protecting our democracy, defending reproductive freedom, combating the climate crisis, and making our economy work for working people. But I don’t want to assume what matters most to you. I want to hear it directly from you. That’s why I’m reaching out today with a quick **2025 Priorities Survey**. It’ll only take a minute or two, but it makes a real difference in shaping how we move forward together. **[Whether your top issue is clean energy, public lands, health care, or something else entirely — I’m listening. Will you take the survey today?](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111046241/556043063/241887131?refcode=AU_MTH_EG_GEN_SUR_AC_20250707_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMTFjZjFlNy02MTViLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lB_RbDhePdzrcGDjfEEOEAO-cxzcbKxp4VtINbbqPoI=&emci=a01cf1e7-615b-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=a11cf1e7-615b-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=252045)** | [TAKE THE SURVEY](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111046242/556043064/241887131?refcode=AU_MTH_EG_GEN_SUR_AC_20250707_PM1_V1_S1&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDYvMS84OTA0NCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICJhMTFjZjFlNy02MTViLWYwMTEtOGY3Yy02MDQ1YmRmZThlOWMiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=lB_RbDhePdzrcGDjfEEOEAO-cxzcbKxp4VtINbbqPoI=&emci=a01cf1e7-615b-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&emdi=a11cf1e7-615b-f011-8f7c-6045bdfe8e9c&ceid=252045)

In case you didn't know, I come from a long line of Virginia trailblazers.

My grandfather was a pioneering Civil Rights Attorney in Norfolk and was the first Black member of the Virginia Board of Education. My father was one of the first students to integrate Norfolk’s public schools, then later went on to serve Virginia in all three branches of government as a Delegate, Director of the Department of Juvenile Justice, and Circuit Court Judge.

To go from the little boy sitting on his dad's lap in General Assembly sessions to later filling that same seat in the House of Delegates is a profound privilege.
_They laid the foundation, and now, I'm ready to build on their legacy._

As the next Attorney General, I will work hand-in-hand with Abigail and Ghazala to make life more affordable for Virginians and secure our rights.

_But first, we have to win._

Winsome Earle-Sears and Jason Miyares have put their allegiance to Trump above the needs of Virginia families. They're backed by a deep bench of donors who'll fund nonstop attack ads in order to keep hold of power.

[_**I mean it, whatever the amount, Abigail and I are counting on your split contribution of $25 or more. It adds up quickly when we all pitch in, so I hope you'll give what you can to become one of our founding donors today.**_](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111046286/556043311/-63596132?refcode=BLU_EM_ASFG_2025-07-07_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNTg2MTFkMGMtNjI1Yi1mMDExLThmN2MtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=69SnS7ZcSxIFi6pr3uwmfI7krwz-VsrAkjnBp6rDvNI=)
| [CONTRIBUTE](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111046287/556043312/-63596132?refcode=BLU_EM_ASFG_2025-07-07_1&amount=25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQMjQvMS8xMDM4NTgiLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiNTg2MTFkMGMtNjI1Yi1mMDExLThmN2MtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=69SnS7ZcSxIFi6pr3uwmfI7krwz-VsrAkjnBp6rDvNI=)

clean_body: I'm going to share my story with you in a moment, but first, I have a small but meaningful request: | = Peter, I'm Jay Jones, the Democratic nominee for Attorney General. I'm honored to join Abigail and Ghazala on the ticket that will flip Virginia blue. I'm going to share my story with you in a moment, but first, I have a small but meaningful request: = | | = | = | = | = | = | = | = In case you didn't know, I come from a long line of Virginia trailblazers. My grandfather was a pioneering Civil Rights Attorney in Norfolk and was the first Black member of the Virginia Board of Education. My father was one of the first students to integrate Norfolk’s public schools, then later went on to serve Virginia in all three branches of government as a Delegate, Director of the Department of Juvenile Justice, and Circuit Court Judge. To go from the little boy sitting on his dad's lap in General Assembly sessions to later filling that same seat in the House of Delegates is a profound privilege. They laid the foundation, and now, I'm ready to build on their legacy. As the next Attorney General, I will work hand-in-hand with Abigail and Ghazala to make life more affordable for Virginians and secure our rights. But first, we have to win. Winsome Earle-Sears and Jason Miyares have put their allegiance to Trump above the needs of Virginia families. They're backed by a deep bench of donors who'll fund nonstop attack ads in order to keep hold of power. = | = Thank you so much, Jay * | | Paid for by Spanberger for Governor Spanberger for Governor P.O. Box 3121 Glen Allen, VA 23058 United States | = =

name: Sen. Jon Ossoff
subject: On authoritarianism
disclaimer_text: Paid For By Gallego for Arizona
body:
MAGA is committed to eliminating Democratic seats in Congress so Trump can continue his “revolution” without domestic opposition.
#

_Our new reality —_
From classrooms to boardrooms to local governments and the press, Americans now fear political retribution from their own government.
Think of the CEO who bends the knee after public threats of prosecution. Or the newspaper owner who decides it’s safer to bury editorial criticism of the President.
The President and his lieutenants relish the power of intimidation. _And they’re using it._
***
Before my election to represent Georgia in the U.S. Senate, I led investigations of corruption and human rights abuses worldwide.
More and more, the atmosphere under Trump reminds me of countries where dissent is silenced by intimidation.
_“We don’t want any trouble.” “We’re not sticking our necks out right now.” “We don’t need an investigation.”_
**These things should never be said in the United States.**
***
During the confirmation hearings for RFK Jr., I noted the Republican Senate’s total capitulation to Trump.
Then they confirmed to lead the FBI (for TEN YEARS) a Trump loyalist who enters office having composed what many call an “enemies list” of MAGA’s adversaries.
_To lead the FBI._
***
Peter: Only by regaining Congressional power can we reverse this.
Remember: in 2026, I’m the only Democratic U.S. Senator up for re-election in a state Trump won.
MAGA needs me defeated and gone.
**_Help me defeat them in a landslide. Help fight back._**
[**Please split $3 between my campaign and Ruben Gallego’s today.](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1000?c0=ruben-ossoff-em-split.vUHoTq&refcode=em250707-1-160&t=1&akid=3913%2E734936%2ECwEtXA)**
[DONATE](https://act.gallegoforarizona.com/go/1000?c0=ruben-ossoff-em-split.vUHoTq&refcode=em250707-1-160&t=2&akid=3913%2E734936%2ECwEtXA)
With gratitude,
Senator Jon Ossoff

name: Hakeem Jeffries
subject: re: One Big Ugly Bill
body:
Extreme House Republicans just approved the largest cut to Medicaid and food assistance in American history to fund tax breaks for their billionaire donors. |

Friend, extreme House Republicans just approved the largest cut to Medicaid and food assistance in American history to fund tax breaks for their billionaire donors.

**The latest GOP stunt will rip healthcare away from 17 MILLION Americans, jack up our utility bills and take food out of the mouths of children, seniors and veterans. It’s disgusting.**

I won’t be silent. You shouldn’t be either.

**[Look, if enough Democrats band together, we can ensure Republicans will always regret this vote. I’m calling for an emergency fundraising surge today – 5,000 Democratic Membership renewals before midnight – to show everyone watching that when Republicans go after our families, we’re coming for their seats. Can I count on you to renew your membership by splitting a donation between me and Derek Tran so we can rush resources to battleground districts, reclaim the House majority and hand the right-wing extremists a stunning rebuke? >>](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/111047521/556047855/-1277880318?refcode=em-7.7.25-all&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNTkvMS8xMDg3ODciLA0KICAiRGlzdHJpYnV0aW9uVW5pcXVlSWQiOiAiOTRjNjM3ZTItNjQ1Yi1mMDExLThmN2MtNjA0NWJkZmU4ZTljIiwNCiAgIkVtYWlsQWRkcmVzcyI6ICJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSINCn0%3D&hmac=UFdmtGaNGR7XtaLFAOU2eI2CrCwJYd1EZG6bL0MaGzY=)**

name: Jason Crow
subject: I didn’t run for Congress to play politics or pad my own pockets
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jason Crow for Congress
body:
Jason Crow For Congress [https://nvlupin.blob.core.windows.net/images/van/NGP/NGP21/1/88523/images/logoJC%20(1).png]
Friend,

I’ve always tried my best to embody the principle of servant leadership.

As an Army Ranger, it meant putting my team first — always. It meant leading by example, carrying more weight, and being the first to jump out of the plane.

That commitment didn’t end when I left the military. When I saw Colorado’s values under threat in Washington, I felt the call to serve again, but this time, in Congress.

Today, I’m still driven by those same values. In my current position, servant leadership means showing up, listening, and fighting every day to make life better for the people I represent, not special interests.

Last week, Republicans in Congress cut health care for millions of Americans. They're gutting Medicaid, defunding critical services, and rewarding their ultra-wealthy allies, while everyday families struggle with the rising cost of care, housing, and child care.

Unlike them, I didn’t come to Congress to play political games or pad my own pockets. I came here to serve and to make sure no one is left behind.

If you’re with me, I hope you’ll consider donating $10 or whatever you can to help sustain this work. [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-july2025?amount=10]

If you've saved your payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation will go through immediately:

DONATE $10 NOW [https://secure.actblue.com/donate/jc-em-july2025?amount=10&express_lane=true]

Thank you for being part of this team.

— Jason

Friends, When President Trump signed the One Big Beautiful Bill into law, I was proud to be standing at his side.
---
---

body:
Cactus Politics Sits Down with KTR

| | | | | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/karrinforarizona/cactuspolitics?e=ebfb0fda52)
---
[|
---](https://karrinforarizona.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=473c144f5f100c2eff990c077&id=99b56fecba&e=ebfb0fda52)
| | | | | **ICYMI: Cactus Politics Karrin Taylor Robson Interview****—————** _Karri n Taylor Robson’s Political Comeback_ _Ericka Pinon | July 7th, 2025_[ _Link to Story_](https://karrinforarizona.us22.list-manage.com/track/click?u=473c144f5f100c2eff990c077&id=65ba115a14&e=ebfb0fda52)

---
| _**From Cactus Politics:**_
Karrin Taylor Robson enters the 2026 Arizona gubernatorial race with significantly stronger positioning than her 2022 campaign, armed with dramatically improved name recognition and a coveted endorsement from President Donald Trump. The business executive and Arizona native has transformed from a relatively unknown candidate to a major political force in the state.
Robson's rise from obscurity to prominence in politics is a remarkable accomplishment. She only had 2% statewide name recognition when she first started her governor campaign in 2022, which presented an immense challenge.
"I often joke about that, that I started from the basement and crawled my way out of the basement," Robson said in a recent interview with Cactus Politics. "I ended up, you know, about 92% name ideas around the state right now, in my race I'm over 80%, which is a huge advantage that they didn't have before."
This dramatic improvement in visibility comes alongside a network of relationships built during her first campaign. "I also met people all over the state and the first run, great people that supported me then and they were stepping up to support me again," she explained, emphasizing the foundation of support that didn't exist in her initial bid.
With President Trump's endorsement, the political landscape has moved substantially in Robson's favor, a striking difference from 2022, when Trump supported her opponent, Kari Lake. The support is particularly significant in Arizona, where Trump won overwhelmingly in 2024, leaving the state solidly red.
"We supported President Trump wholeheartedly in 2016, 2020, and 2024. I was very, very honored, this time to get the president's endorsement," Robson said. "And I'm going to do everything I can, when elected to have President Trump's back to support the things he was elected to do, and Arizona voters overwhelmingly voted for President Trump."
Robson positions herself as sharing Donald Trump's executive background, drawing parallels between their business experience. "Like President Trump, I'm a business executive. He knows Arizona's a red state and he knows what it takes to be a leader and be an executive," she stated, emphasizing that voters want "an executive with business experience to lead your state as the chief executive of the state."
**Housing Crisis** Robson has made housing affordability a central issue, promising to lift what she describes as Governor Katie Hobbs' growth moratorium in high-growth Phoenix areas. She argues this policy created a housing crisis that has made homeownership unaffordable for many Arizona families, positioning it as her top priority from day one in office.
"The first thing she did when she came into office was to create a housing crisis by instituting a moratorium on growth in some of the high growth parts of the Phoenix Metropolitan area," Robson explained. "And one of the first things I will do as governor is to lift that growth moratorium, so that we can get more housing into the market, to make it more affordable for our own families. I think that's number one."
The housing crisis represents what Robson sees as a direct government-created problem with a clear solution. "His many Arizona families cannot afford a house today. She created that problem, and we will undo that," she declared, emphasizing that this would be an immediate reversal rather than a gradual policy shift. Robson sees the housing crisis as an example of larger government overreach that suppresses economic growth and harms working people who are currently dealing with affordability issues in numerous sectors.
**Border Security** Citing statistics that 85% of fentanyl entering the U.S. crosses through Arizona, Robson has made border security a priority, praising Trump's immigration enforcement success while positioning herself as a collaborative partner rather than an obstacle to federal efforts. She highlighted Trump's remarkable achievement, claiming illegal crossings dropped from 64,000 in May 2023 to zero in May 2024.
"President Trump has done a phenomenal job if you're watching the numbers at all. Just in May, a year ago, there were 64,000 crossings on the illegal crossings on our southern border. In May of this year, there was zero. President Trump did that without a new law, without being told to do it. He did what he told the American public that he would do," Robson stated.
She contrasts her approach with Governor Hobbs' response to federal immigration enforcement, arguing that Hobbs has actively undermined border security efforts. "She's also been standing in the way of President Trump's efforts to support secure the border, keep the border secure, and she's also kind of held the, you know, tied the hands of our local law enforcement who President Trump needs to support his efforts in federal officials' efforts to get the violent criminals that have crossed the border illegally out of this country."
Robson emphasizes the ongoing threat posed by criminal organizations, warning that "cartels or our multibillion dollar business enterprise. They're not just going to, you know, walk away. They're going to find ways to continue to bring their drugs and destruction into our country." She promises to work collaboratively with federal authorities: "I will work with President Trump, not against President Trump to make sure those borders stay secure, and that we can stop the flow of illegal smuggling of people and drugs."
The candidate takes a hard line on criminal aliens, echoing Trump's messaging about countries emptying their institutions. "President Trump has made it very clear that a lot of these countries emptied their prisons. They emptied their mental health institutions and sent those people across our borders. That's unacceptable," she said, promising to tell unwelcome criminals: "You're not welcome in Arizona. You are not welcome, because the cost to Arizona's is in the billions of dollars. That is not fair to hard work in Arizona and hard work in Arizona families."
**Education Reform** While complimenting Arizona's school choice programs, Robson cited third-grade comprehension as a major issue requiring quick attention. She stated that Arizona's rates are in the 30th percentile compared to the national average of 60%, with some districts performing in the single digits, a scenario she views intolerable for the state's students.
"Arizona has done a really good job in certain areas when it comes to education. In particular school choice and parental involvement, school choice, we have more kids enrolled in charter schools per capita than any other state and nation," Robson acknowledged, pointing to the state's strong performance in some areas. "We have some of the best performing schools in the country. You look at the top ten public high schools in the nation and every year I can think of for the last decade plus, Arizona has had high schools on that list. Oftentimes more than one high school on that list."
However, she identified a stark failure in fundamental skills that undermines these achievements. "However, across the board where we're failing in certain areas, in particular in reading, I plan to focus specifically on third grade reading," she explained. "Currently Arizona's third grade reading proficiency lags the nation, and the nation's third grade reading proficiency is only in the 60% percentile, but in Arizona, it's in the 30s. And in some of our districts, it's in the single digits."
Robson's approach centers on a critical educational principle that she wants to be universally understood. "I want every parent, every student, every teacher, every administrator to understand and talk about the importance of third grade reading if we spend the first three years reading to learn and the rest of our life learning to read," she stated. "And if we can't get our kids to third grade reading proficiency, they're going to continue to fall behind."
She promises to implement accountability measures that refuse to advance struggling students without mastering basic skills. "I want every third grade student in Arizona to know how to read. They will become a fourth grader and let them know how to read in third grade," Robson declared. "And for me, that's unacceptable. It's not fair to that individual child, because we are doing a disservice to keep passing them along that they don't know how to read."
**Government Efficiency** Robson plans to create an "Arizona DOGE" to eliminate government waste and establish a Governor's Office of Congressional Accountability to better coordinate the state's federal delegation, promising immediate action on both fronts starting from day one in office.
"In my first 100 days, and actually day one, what I intend to do is start our own Arizona Doge," Robson announced. "We are going to do everything we can to eliminate waste fraud and abuse in the Arizona government, because every dollar that is wasted, misplaced, or fraudulently spent is a dollar out of a taxpayer's pocket. And when affordability is such an issue for Arizona families now, every dollar we say is a dollar they can keep in their pocket."
Beyond state government efficiency, Robson plans to address what she sees as a lack of coordination among Arizona's federal representatives. "I will also start in a Governor's Office of Congressional Accountability because I don't believe our congressional delegation works like a cohesive unit in the best interests of the state of Arizona," she explained. "Members of our delegation, they each have their little pet projects and they answer to their constituents as they should, but nobody is holding their feet to the fire on major federal issues that affect Arizona."
Her vision for congressional accountability involves prioritizing statewide issues over individual districts. "And so I intend to help our congressional delegation prioritize those issues so that collectively, they can better serve the residents of Arizona," she stated. The approach reflects her business background in managing complex organizations and coordinating different departments toward common goals.
Robson emphasized that government efficiency directly impacts family finances during challenging economic times. "So we will spend an awful lot of energy, early on, to make sure we are eliminating waste fraud and abuse," she promised, connecting taxpayer savings to household affordability challenges that Arizona families currently face.
Robson identified a basic economic disadvantage that Arizona faces as a result of federal land ownership patterns, an issue that receives little attention but has a considerable influence on the state's revenue base and economic growth.
"Most of Arizona's land is owned by the, you know, public federal government, state government, and Indian communities. Federal government alone owns millions and millions of acres of land," she explained. "So we have to carry the burden of that land, and it doesn't produce any taxes."
The statistics reveal Arizona's disadvantage compared to other major states. "In Arizona, 83% of our land is publicly owned, so only 17% of our land is owned by individuals paying taxes. Compare that to a state like Texas or Florida, where 95% of the land is paying taxes."
This disparity creates ongoing financial challenges for Arizona taxpayers. "When Arizona became a state, the federal government paid its payment in lieu of taxes, that formula for all that federal lands, so that the taxpayers of Arizona could alleviate some of that burden. That formula has not been updated in decades. So Arizona's got screwed."
**Executive Experience and Conservative Values** Robson consistently emphasizes her business background as a key differentiator from career politicians, arguing that executive experience translates directly to effective governance. "I've managed and dealt with a lot of challenges throughout my career, getting things built, getting things approved, getting things through, you know, through the red tape of government, which is not always an easy task to do."
Her management philosophy centers on bringing people together to solve problems. "I've been able to, you know, accept those challenges and work through them. And oftentimes that means bringing people together to find the best solution to things. And those are skills that executives bring to the table."
Beyond management skills, Robson emphasizes the visionary aspect of executive leadership. "The executive also can define a vision, articulate the vision, and inspire people to follow them. And that's what I've done my entire life, and that's what I intend to do is govern."Her conservative credentials span the full spectrum of Republican priorities. "I am a conservative. I always have been, always will believe. You know, I've been a pro life supporter, I've been, you support the First Amendment, the Second Amendment Amendment. I believe our rights flow from God and not the government. I believe in a low tax, low regulatory environment."
**Sharp Contrast with Governor Hobbs** Robson sharply contrasts Governor Hobbs, describing her as fundamentally opposed to Arizona's conservative ideals. "Katie Hobbs does not share those values. Katie Hobbs is a radical. She is a lefty, and the only thing standing between, you know, Arizona and turning us into California is having the Republican majorities in our state legislature."The criticism extends beyond policy disagreements to questions of legal compliance. "What we know is that Katie Hobbs is radical, right? She is currently under investigation for corruption by our Democrat attorney General. And so Katie Hobson, unfortunately, for the people of Arizona, is on the wrong side of the law more often than not."
Robson argues that without Republican legislative majorities, "Katie Hobbs would turn Arizona into California," emphasizing that "Arizona's a conservative state, and they share my values, pro life, First Amendment, Second Amendment, go down the list of things that matter to conservatives and that are conservative values."
Robson plans aggressive regulatory reform to reduce burdens on Arizona businesses and families. "I would fight tooth and nail to keep Arizona's tax burden low and our regulatory burden low," she stated, proposing a comprehensive review of existing regulations."Institute a moratorium on any new regulations until we have another thorough review of the regulations here on the books," she explained. "You know, the government regulations over time tend to snowball and just get more and more and more. And I think it's time again, to put the brakes on that."
Her approach emphasizes questioning the government's role across all areas. "We should question everything the government does to make sure, is it needed? Is it something we should continue to do or should we eliminate it? Sometimes it's better to eliminate."
**Personal Motivation and Arizona's Appeal** Robson's campaign is deeply personal, rooted in her lifelong connection to Arizona and her experience traveling the state during her first campaign. "I learned how much more I love this state than I thought I did. It is more beautiful, and I'm an Arizona native. I grew up here, and I've always known Arizona's a beautiful state."
Her travels revealed not just Arizona's natural beauty but the character of its people. "But more importantly is the people. I love meeting people, I learn a lot from meeting people in Arizona. It is full of really, really good people here. You know, God Fearing patriots, hardworking people that literally want the government out of their lives."
The candidate sees Arizona's population as uniquely freedom-oriented. "72% of our adults came from somewhere else. They're attracted to this place because of freedom," she explained, noting that people moved to Arizona "because of freedom" and want to "fulfill their God given estimate."
**Unchanged Core Values** Despite the political journey from unknown candidate to major contender, Robson emphasizes that her fundamental values remain constant. "My fundamental values have not changed. I am a mom, a grown up, a wife, I've lived the American dream."
Her sense of responsibility drives her continued involvement in politics. "We're only here for a very short time, and we owe it to the people that came before us, that gave us the American privilege that we enjoy, and they have to pass that on," she explained. "And during our very short time here, I believe, we have a responsibility to do everything we can to pass that legacy on to the next generations."
**Looking Ahead to 2026** With Governor Hobbs declaring her reelection candidacy on Tuesday, Robson's early campaign start positions her to gain traction from her Trump endorsement and drastically boosted name recognition. Her campaign builds on themes from her 2022 campaign, but with substantially stronger positioning and a more defined policy platform.
The race promises to be a clear ideological choice between Robson's conservative, business-focused approach and Hobbs' progressive governance style. Robson's early start, combined with her Trump endorsement and established statewide network, suggests she aims to avoid the primary challenges that complicated her 2022 campaign while building a broader coalition for the general election.
Arizona's political landscape in 2026 will likely center on the themes Robson has already begun emphasizing: housing affordability, border security, education reform, and government efficiency. Her positioning as a Trump-endorsed conservative with executive experience offers a clear contrast to Hobbs' progressive record and sets up what promises to be one of the most closely watched gubernatorial races in the country.
